title: Indian-inspired Aerated Canadian Beef Burger
description: A unique, easy-to-make burger featuring an aerated Canadian beef patty, topped with tandoori spices and served in a mason jar.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, all-purpose flour, salt, and ground cloves.
Mix the beef mixture until it's well combined.
Add the tandoori spice mix and mix well.
Using your hands, shape the beef mixture into 4 patties.
Place the patties on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the patties in the preheated oven for 15 minutes.
While the patties are baking, toast the buns on the oven rack.
In a small bowl, mix the beef broth with the mayonnaise.
Assemble the burgers by spreading the beef broth-mayonnaise mixture on the bottom bun, followed by a beef patty, some onion, tomato, and cucumber, and finally the top bun.
Garnish with additional tandoori spice mix if desired.
Serve the burgers in mason jars.
